steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $-6c^3 + 5c + 15$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content: Start with $$(5c+8)-(6c^3-7).$$ Subtracting the second parentheses changes both signs, so the expression becomes $$5c+8-6c^3+7.$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: The cubic term is $-6c^3$, the linear term is $5c$, and the constants combine to $8+7=15$. So the expression is $$-6c^3+5c+15.$$
